blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
The card game **Blind Man's Bluff** is also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker** (where you can see everyone else's cards but your own). 

Several real, existing Android apps on the Google Play Store offer this specific game style:

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game** (by hheeee)
* **Overview:** This is a highly polished, single-player psychological card game built entirely around the rules of Blind Man's Bluff. 
* **Features:** You play offline against an AI opponent. Since you can only see the AI's card, you have to read its "expressions" and betting patterns to guess the value of your own card. It features multiple difficulty levels, customizable avatars, and a betting system (Call, Half, Fold/Die, or All-In).

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Overview:** A multiplayer-focused version of the game designed for playing with friends and family.
* **Features:** The app lets you play the classic "forehead card" style game where you must predict your victory based solely on your opponent's card. It emphasizes reading your opponent's expressions and managing your psychological strategy.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Overview:** A lightweight, straightforward simulation of the game.
* **Features:** This app is designed to act as a digital deck for multiple people wanting to play the game together in person. It is simple, ad-supported, and gets straight to the gameplay.

### 4. **Anipang Poker / 애니팡 포커** (by Playlinks)
* **Overview:** A massive social casino and poker app that includes several game modes.
* **Features:** Alongside traditional 7-card poker and Badugi, it features a dedicated **Indian Poker** (Blind Man's Bluff) game mode where you can challenge other players online or play with friends.

---

### ⚠️ A Quick Tip on Searching:
If you search the Play Store, be careful not to confuse "Blind Man's Bluff" with apps simply named **"Bluff"** (such as *Bluff Card Game* by Oengines or Oneiric Gaming). Those apps are actually digital versions of **Cheat** (also known as *I Doubt It* or *BS*), which is a completely different card discarding game. Searching for **"Indian Poker"** or **"Blindex"** on the Play Store will yield the exact gameplay style you are looking for.

forehead poker app Android.response:
Depending on what you mean by "forehead poker," you might be looking for the actual **card game** (also known as *Indian Poker* or *Blind Man's Bluff*, where you hold a playing card to your forehead and bet based on everyone else's cards) or the popular **word-guessing party games** played by holding your phone to your forehead. 

The best Android apps for both styles of the game include:

### 1. Indian Poker / Blind Man's Bluff (The Card Game)
If you want to play the classic card game where you see everyone's card but your own:

*   **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
    This app is a direct digital adaptation of the classic Indian Poker game. It allows you to play with friends and family, testing your psychological reading skills as you try to predict your victory solely by looking at your opponents' cards.
*   **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
    A straightforward, lightweight simulation app designed to let multiple people play the traditional card-on-forehead game using digital playing cards. 

---

### 2. Forehead Word Guessing Games (Heads Up / Charades)
If you are looking for the party game where you place the phone on your forehead and guess the word on the screen based on your friends' clues:

*   **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
    The official, sensationally popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. You choose a deck (ranging from celebrities and movies to custom categories), place the phone on your forehead, and tilt it up or down to pass or mark a correct guess.
*   **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** (by Cosmicode)
    A highly-rated alternative to Heads Up that features team modes, custom category creators, and the ability to record video of your friends acting out clues so you can watch and share the hilarious highlights later.
*   **Charades & Headbands Game** (by What am I?)
    A fun, family-friendly guessing game with over 60 themed decks. It features simple "tilt-to-play" mechanics and works perfectly for gatherings, parties, or casual game nights.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
If you are looking for an Android app where you cannot see your own cards but can see everyone else's, this style of gameplay is typically found in games like **Indian Poker** (also known as **Blind Man's Bluff**) or the cooperative game **Hanabi**. 

The following real, existing Android apps on the Google Play Store fit this description:

### 1. Blindex - Blind Card Game
* **Description:** This is a single-player psychological card game based directly on Blind Man's Bluff (Indian Poker). In this game, you are dealt a card face-down that you cannot see, but your AI opponent's card is shown face-up. You must read the AI's facial expressions, tells, and betting patterns to guess the value of your own card and decide whether to call, fold, or go all-in. It features offline play and multiple difficulty levels.

### 2. Indian Poker - Card Game (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Description:** A multiplayer-focused implementation of Indian Poker designed to be played with friends and family. Just like the physical party game, you have to predict your victory by looking only at your opponents' cards. It relies heavily on reading the psychology and expressions of the other players.

### 3. Indian poker (by Kanbe)
* **Description:** A straightforward, clean simulation app that lets you play Indian Poker using a virtual deck of playing cards. It is designed to be played by multiple people on mobile devices, making it a great digital substitute for a physical deck of cards when playing the "forehead card" game in a group.

### 4. Board Game Arena (via Web/Android)
* **Description:** While not a standalone app, Board Game Arena is fully optimized for Android and hosts the official digital version of **Hanabi**—the world's most famous cooperative card game where you cannot see your own hand. In Hanabi, players hold their cards facing outward. You must work together to launch a spectacular fireworks display by giving each other clues about what cards they are holding.

The top Android apps available on the Google Play Store for this game include:

### 1. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)

* **Features:** It features over 60 pre-made decks covering anime, video games, TV shows, and music, alongside a custom deck creator.

### 4. **Heads Up! Netflix Edition** (by Netflix, Inc.)

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some of the best real, offline, single-player bluffing card games available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Bluff Card Game Offline**
* **What it is:** A dedicated offline version of the classic card game also known as *Cheat*, *I Doubt It*, or *BS*. 
* **Why it fits:** It is designed specifically to be played without an internet connection. You can play in 2-player (1v1) or 4-player modes against smart AI opponents that have dynamic difficulty. The goal is to get rid of all your cards by playing them face-down and declaring their rank—whether you are telling the truth or bluffing.
* **Key Features:** Smooth card animations, daily bonus rewards, and offline stat tracking to see how your bluffing skills improve over time.

### 2. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game**
* **What it is:** Another excellent adaptation of the classic *Cheat* card game, built for quick offline sessions.
* **Why it fits:** It features intense 4-player battles where you play against three CPU opponents. The game uses a unique system that tracks your playstyle, labeling you as a "Liar," "Honest," or "Hunter".
* **Key Features:** It adds a fun twist to the classic rules by introducing strategic items like a "Lie Counter" and "Bluff Shield" to help you outsmart the AI.

### 3. **CleverFox - I Doubt It**
* **What it is:** A highly customizable version of the classic *Cheat* / *I Doubt It* card game.
* **Why it fits:** While it does have online modes, it features a robust offline single-player mode where you can challenge computer-controlled opponents. 
* **Key Features:** It allows you to customize the rules before starting (such as playing same rank, lower card, upper card, or random card), keeping the offline gameplay fresh and strategically diverse.

### 4. **Love Letter - Strategy Card Game**
* **What it is:** The official digital adaptation of the award-winning physical card game *Love Letter*.
* **Why it fits:** *Love Letter* is a brilliant game of deduction, risk, and bluffing. You only hold one or two cards at a time and must use character abilities to eliminate other suitors or protect your own letter. It features a dedicated offline single-player mode where you play against computer AI.
* **Key Features:** Beautiful artwork, quick 5-to-10-minute rounds, and highly strategic gameplay where reading your opponents' bluffs is key to winning.

best offline single player card game And.response:
Here are some of the best offline, single-player card games available on the Google Play Store, ranging from tactical roguelikes to unique puzzle card games:

### 1. **Balatro**
* **The Vibe:** A poker-themed roguelike deckbuilder.
* **Why it’s great:** *Balatro* is one of the most addictive card games ever made. Instead of fighting monsters, your goal is to play illegal poker hands to score points and beat "blinds" (bosses). You buy and collect over 150 unique Joker cards, each of which grants game-changing multipliers and synergies. It is a premium, fully offline game that is perfect for long sessions.

### 2. **Slay the Spire**
* **The Vibe:** The gold standard of roguelike deckbuilders.
* **Why it’s great:** You choose one of four distinct characters, each with their own unique card pool, and attempt to climb a procedurally generated tower. Along the way, you draft cards, fight bizarre monsters, and collect powerful relics that alter your gameplay. It is incredibly deep, highly replayable, and runs flawlessly offline.

### 3. **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
* **The Vibe:** A dark fantasy, story-driven deckbuilder built specifically for mobile.
* **Why it’s great:** Unlike many PC ports, *Dawncaster* is played in portrait mode, making it perfect for one-handed play. It blends classic RPG progression with deckbuilding. You choose a class (like Warrior, Rogue, or Seeker), gather cards, and make choices that shape your journey. It is a premium game with zero intrusive microtransactions and works entirely offline.

### 4. **Card Thief**
* **The Vibe:** A stealth puzzle game played with a deck of cards.
* **Why it’s great:** You play as a thief moving through a 3x3 grid of cards. Your goal is to sneak past guards, extinguish torches, pickpocket targets, and escape with the treasure chest. It is a highly tactical, grid-based puzzle game that requires careful planning of your path. It is free to download with a one-time purchase to unlock the full game.

### 5. **Pirates Outlaws**
* **The Vibe:** A pirate-themed roguelike card game.
* **Why it’s great:** Similar to *Slay the Spire*, but with a distinct pirate aesthetic and unique mechanics. You manage your "ammo" to play cards, recruit a crew, and navigate through different maps to defeat outlaw bosses. It features a massive roster of playable characters, hundreds of cards to collect, and multiple game modes that can be played offline.

### 6. **Night of the Full Moon**
* **The Vibe:** A dark, fairy-tale-themed deckbuilder.
* **Why it’s great:** Based on the story of Little Red Riding Hood, this game has you fighting through a dark forest to find your missing grandmother. It features beautiful artwork, a vertical layout, and several distinct classes (like Ranger, Nun, or Alchemist) that completely change how you play. It is free to try with optional paid DLCs for extra classes and story chapters.

best offline single player card game And.response:
If you want the **best offline single-player card games on Android**, the strongest picks are **Solitaire Grand Harvest**, **Spider Solitaire**, and **Shadow Era** for different play styles. These are all real Android apps and can be played without an internet connection after installation.

- **Solitaire Grand Harvest** — a polished **single-player Solitaire** game with level progression and a long offline-friendly campaign.
- **Spider Solitaire** — the classic **single-player card puzzle**; good if you want a simple, no-frills offline game.
- **Hearthstone** — not fully offline for everything, but it includes a strong **solo card-game experience**; best if you want a deeper collectible-card feel.
- **Shadow Era** — a full **digital trading card game** with solo play options and a more strategic, competitive style.
- **Solitaire Decked Out** — another solid **offline Solitaire** option with clean presentation and straightforward gameplay.

If you want the **single best all-around offline choice**, I’d start with **Spider Solitaire** for pure offline reliability or **Solitaire Grand Harvest** if you want more progression and polish.
